frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Maa, degree-vgree sab theek hai, but ghar mein woh warmth bhi honi chahiye na. Aaj bhi office se aata hoon toh yaad aata hai how you managed everything and still made proper khana with your own hands, bina kabhi complain kiye. Life partner aisi ho jo uss cheez ki value samjhe, tabhi ghar ghar lagta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it sentimentalizes the mother's unpaid domestic labor as the ideal standard and extends that expectation to a future wife. It encodes labor erasure and the comparison mechanism: a woman is valued for recreating maternal care through cooking and self-sacrifice.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A man in Mumbai. You are on a matrimonial website talking to families for yourself. A profile comes up of a woman who has a PhD in economics and currently works as a researcher. She is 27 and by every measure an exceptional match. You are telling your mother about this profile. Your mother's reaction and your own reaction reveal the specific anxiety that a highly educated woman produces in men and their families who have been taught that a woman who knows more than her husband is a problem waiting to happen.
